words.def: the leader of a republic, for example the US
words.example: Several presidents attended the funeral.

words.def: newspapers and magazines
words.example: the local/national/foreign press

words.def: the act of trying to persuade or to force somebody to do something
words.example: pressure for something, The pressure for change continued to mount.

words.def: happening or existing before the event or object that you are talking about
words.example: No previous experience is necessary for this job.

words.def: a person who is qualified to perform religious duties and ceremonies in the Roman Catholic, Anglican and Orthodox Churches
words.example: a parish priest

words.def: a male member of a royal family who is not king, especially the son or grandson of the king or queen
words.example: the royal princes

words.def: a female member of a royal family who is not a queen, especially the daughter or granddaughter of the king or queen
words.example: the royal princesses

words.def: the act of producing letters, pictures, patterns, etc. on something by pressing a surface covered with ink (= coloured liquid for printing, drawing, etc.) against it
words.example: the invention of printing

words.def: a building where people are kept as a punishment for a crime they have committed, or while they are waiting for trial
words.example: She went to prison for tax evasion.

words.def: a person who is kept in prison as a punishment, or while they are waiting for trial
words.example: The number of prisoners serving life sentences has fallen.

words.def: belonging to or for the use of a particular person or group; not for public use
words.example: The sign said, ‘Private property. Keep out.’

words.def: an award that is given to a person who wins a competition, race, etc. or who does very good work
words.example: She was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.

words.def: a person, a company or a country that grows or makes food, goods or materials
words.example: French wine producers

words.def: the process of growing or making food, goods or materials, especially large quantities
words.example: food/oil production

words.def: a type of job that needs special training or skill, especially one that needs a high level of education
words.example: the medical/legal/teaching profession

words.def: a university teacher of the highest rank
words.example: Professor (Ann) Williams

words.def: a set of instructions in code that control the operations or functions of a computer
words.example: You'll need to download and install the program on your computer.

words.def: to help sell a product, service, etc. or make it more popular by advertising it or offering it at a special price
words.example: promote something, The band has gone on tour to promote their new album.

words.def: right, appropriate or correct; according to the rules
words.example: We should have had a proper discussion before voting.

words.def: in a way that is correct and/or appropriate
words.example: How much money do we need to do the job properly?

words.def: a thing or things that are owned by somebody; a possession or possessions
words.example: personal/stolen property

words.def: to make sure that somebody/something is not harmed, injured, damaged, etc.
words.example: protect somebody/something/yourself, to protect children/citizens/civilians/consumers/workers

words.def: the expression of strong dislike of or opposition to something; a statement or an action that shows this
words.example: to hold/organize/join a protest

words.def: feeling pleased and satisfied about something that you own or have done, or are connected with
words.example: proud parents

words.def: to use facts, evidence, etc. to show that something is true
words.example: prove something, They hope this new evidence will prove her innocence.

words.def: a building where people go to drink and meet their friends. Pubs serve alcoholic and other drinks, and often also food.
words.example: We're all going to the pub after work.

words.def: connected with ordinary people in society in general
words.example: The campaign is designed to increase public awareness of the issues.

words.def: an act of trying to make something move by taking hold of it and using force to bring it towards you
words.example: I gave the door a sharp pull and it opened.

words.def: to make somebody suffer because they have broken the law or done something wrong
words.example: punish somebody, Those responsible for this crime will be severely punished.

words.def: an act or a way of punishing somebody
words.example: to inflict/impose/mete out punishment

words.def: an act of pushing something/somebody
words.example: She gave him a gentle push.

words.def: having passed the exams or completed the training that are necessary in order to do a particular job; having the experience to do a particular job
words.example: a qualified teacher

words.def: to reach the standard of ability or knowledge needed to do a particular job, for example by completing a course of study or passing exams
words.example: How long does it take to qualify?

words.def: the standard of something when it is compared to other things like it; how good or bad something is
words.example: Improving the quality of care for nursing home residents is a priority.

words.def: an amount or a number of something
words.example: quantity of something, a large/small quantity of something

words.def: a line of people, cars, etc. waiting for something or to do something
words.example: There were long queues at polling stations.

words.def: a competition between people, animals, vehicles, etc. to see which one is the faster or fastest
words.example: a boat/horse race

words.def: the sport of racing horses
words.example: He used to watch the racing on TV in the afternoons.

words.def: a track with rails on which trains run
words.example: The railway is still under construction.

words.def: not done, seen, happening, etc. very often
words.example: a rare disease/occurrence/event

words.def: not very often
words.example: She is rarely seen in public nowadays.

words.def: to change or behave in a particular way as a result of or in response to something
words.example: I nudged her but she didn't react.

words.def: the true situation and the problems that actually exist in life, in contrast to how you would like life to be
words.example: She refuses to face reality.

words.def: a piece of paper or an electronic document that shows that goods or services have been paid for
words.example: Can I have a receipt, please?

words.def: the area inside the entrance of a hotel, an office building, etc. where guests or visitors go first when they arrive
words.example: Please report to reception on arrival.

words.def: to know who somebody is or what something is when you see or hear them or it, because you have seen or heard them or it before
words.example: recognize somebody/something, I recognized him as soon as he came in the room.

words.def: to tell somebody that something is good or useful, or that somebody would be suitable for a particular job, etc.
words.example: recommend somebody/something, The hotel's new restaurant comes highly recommended (= a lot of people have praised it).

words.def: a written account of something that is kept so that it can be looked at and used in the future
words.example: You should keep a record of your expenses.

words.def: sound or pictures that have been recorded on computer files or on CD, DVD, video, etc.
words.example: an audio recording

words.def: to make something less or smaller in size, quantity, price, etc.; to become less or smaller in size, quantity, etc.
words.example: reduce something, Reduce speed now (= on a sign).

words.def: to show the image of somebody/something on the surface of something such as a mirror, water or glass
words.example: be reflected (in something), His face was reflected in the mirror.

words.def: to say or show that you will not do something that somebody has asked you to do
words.example: Go on, ask her; she can hardly refuse.

words.def: a large area of land, usually without exact limits or borders
words.example: mountainous/coastal regions

words.def: at regular intervals or times
words.example: We meet regularly to discuss the progress of the project.

words.def: to refuse to accept or consider something
words.example: to reject an argument/a hypothesis/a notion/a plan

words.def: show or make a connection between two or more things
words.example: relate something, I found it difficult to relate the two ideas in my mind.

words.def: the way in which two people, groups or countries behave towards each other or deal with each other
words.example: diplomatic/international/foreign relations

words.def: the way in which two people, groups or countries behave towards each other or deal with each other
words.example: a personal/working relationship

words.def: considered and judged by being compared with something else
words.example: You must consider the relative merits of the two plans.

words.def: calm and not anxious or worried
words.example: He appeared relaxed and confident before the match.

words.def: helping you to rest and become less anxious
words.example: a relaxing evening with friends

words.def: the act of setting a person or an animal free; the state of being set free
words.example: release of somebody/something, The government has been working to secure the release of the hostages.

words.def: that can be trusted to do something well; that you can rely on
words.example: We are looking for someone who is reliable and hard-working.

words.def: the belief in the existence of a god or gods, and the activities that are connected with the worship of them, or in the teachings of a spiritual leader
words.example: Is there always a conflict between science and religion?

words.def: connected with religion or with a particular religion
words.example: religious beliefs/convictions/faith

words.def: to continue to be something; to be still in the same state or condition
words.example: remain + adj., to remain silent

words.def: to help somebody remember something, especially something important that they must do
words.example: remind somebody/yourself, I'm sorry, I've forgotten your name. Can you remind me?

words.def: far away from places where other people live
words.example: a remote village/island/location/region

words.def: to take somebody/something away from a place
words.example: remove somebody/something, Illegally parked vehicles will be removed.

words.def: an amount of money that you regularly pay so that you can use a house, room, etc.
words.example: I earn just about enough to pay the rent.

words.def: an act of repairing something
words.example: They agreed to pay the costs of any repairs.

words.def: an event that is very similar to something that happened before
words.example: repeat of something, She didn't want a repeat performance of what had happened the night before.

words.def: happening, said or done many times
words.example: repeated absences from work

words.def: to be a member of a group of people and act or speak for them at an event, a meeting, etc.
words.example: The competition attracted over 500 contestants representing eight different countries.

words.def: the action of asking for something formally and politely; a thing that you formally ask for
words.example: request for something, a request for information

words.def: to need something; to depend on somebody/something
words.example: require something, These pets require a lot of care and attention.

words.def: a person who studies something carefully and tries to discover new facts about it
words.example: European researchers say olive oil could help prevent cancer.

words.def: an arrangement for a seat on a plane or train, a room in a hotel, etc. to be kept for you
words.example: I'll call the restaurant and make a reservation.

words.def: a supply of something that a country, an organization or a person has and can use, especially to increase their wealth
words.example: the exploitation of minerals and other natural resources

words.def: a strong feeling of approval of somebody/something because of their good qualities or achievements
words.example: I have the utmost respect for her and her work.

words.def: to give a spoken or written answer to somebody/something
words.example: I asked him his name, but he didn't respond.

words.def: a duty to deal with or take care of somebody/something, so that you may be blamed if something goes wrong
words.example: to be in a position of responsibility

words.def: having the job or duty of doing something or taking care of somebody/something, so that you may be blamed if something goes wrong
words.example: responsible for doing something, Mike is responsible for designing the entire project.

words.def: the part of something that remains
words.example: Take what you want and throw the rest away.

words.def: a thing that is caused or produced because of something else
words.example: And did your intervention produce the desired result?
